FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Thin banking news day—prediction markets hit records on World Cup betting, while STMicro and Soitec file routine disclosures.

bullets.text: Prediction market platforms Kalshi, Polymarket, and Rothera hit record June volumes on World Cup betting, with Rothera alone moving $2B—shows retail appetite for event-driven trading.
STMicroelectronics announced Q2 2026 earnings timing (appears in both English and French releases); no M&A or capital events flagged.
Soitec filed routine French market disclosure on voting rights and share capital composition—no material events to report.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Vestmark's TAMP platform hits a critical inflection point with $50B AUM; separately, Stansberry outlines SMA product strategies.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP platform crossed $50B AUM (5x growth) after launching retail UMA with tax management; T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, and VanEck now referring clients, signaling major demand for standalone RIA tech.
Stansberry Asset Management highlighted two SMA strategies (All-Weather and Tactical Select) in product-focused interview; no material M&A, fundraising, or regulatory developments.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Reinsurance market faces sustained pricing pressure through 2027 despite sidecar growth, while underwriting automation accelerates industry-wide.

bullets.text: Sidecars hit $90bn in reserves (up 64% since 2023), fueled by US annuity demand—but JP Morgan sees no floor on reinsurance pricing through mid-2027 due to oversupply and light hurricane season.
US property mid-year renewals show reinsurers offering flexible structures and customization—a buyer's market signal despite the price compression headwind.
40% of insurers now deploy AI in underwriting as competitive pressure drives automation adoption across the sector.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Four stories spanning stablecoin infrastructure, regulatory warnings on tokenization risk, and emerging AI governance frameworks—plus a €15bn direct lending close.

bullets.text: Standard Chartered + Circle: Institutional clients now get direct USDC minting/redemption access, signaling major bank embrace of stablecoin plumbing for digital asset trading.
IMF sounds alarm on tokenization shifting financial risk from regulated banks to less-supervised infrastructure providers—potential game-changer for regulatory frameworks.
Singapore MAS + banks publish AI agent safeguards white paper, early move to build regulatory guardrails around autonomous systems before the rush accelerates.
Hayfin closes €15bn fifth direct lending fund with more conservative structuring, pivoting after US semi-liquid vehicle headwinds.

PE & Deals.lede: Evercore bulges into Asia secondaries, Sandbrook deploys €200M into European data centers, and institutional capital processes continue their usual cadence.

bullets.text: Evercore hired a secondaries specialist from HarbourVest to lead Asia ops, replacing departing Singapore director—bulge-bracket shops continue muscle-flexing in alternatives and LP secondaries.
Sandbrook deployed €200M into Krios, a European data-center platform, as PE continues chasing AI compute demand in digital infrastructure.
Wellesley Retirement Board (Massachusetts) issued RFP for investment consultant to manage pension/OPEB assets—routine institutional capital deployment process underway.

Markets & Macro.lede: Tech valuations under pressure amid market repricing, while earnings forecasts surge and bond markets signal potential Fed pivot—but breadth concerns and deal friction persist.

bullets.text: KKR/Bridgepoint's £5.7bn DCC Energy bid faces major shareholder opposition from Ninety One, Aviva, and Fidelity—classic PE deal friction ahead.
S&P 500 earnings forecasts rising at fastest pace since 2020, sparking 'earnings bubble' concerns as valuations disconnect from fundamentals.
June jobs miss (217k vs 272k revised May) + narrowing breadth signal structural shift: AI trades concentrating gains while bond yields reset lower, potential Fed rate-cut catalyst.
Tech valuation deterioration signals broader market repricing; old-fashioned valuation discipline likely to outperform in downturn ahead.

Newsletters.lede: Marc Rubinstein examines valuation stretches in today's market environment.

bullets.text: Net Interest dives into widening valuation gaps and what they signal for risk positioning in equities.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four macro trends reshaping developed economies: demographic shifts boosting per-capita growth, infrastructure decay in wealthy nations, pharmaceutical interventions altering behavior at scale, and AI accelerating idea production.

bullets.text: Falling birth rates correlate with higher per-capita GDP growth, challenging conventional wisdom that demographic decline harms economies.
Britain's per-capita income now matches Mississippi's—driven by underinvestment in infrastructure and services, not statistical quirks.
GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic may reshape psychology, ambition, and risk-taking behavior at population scale beyond weight loss.
AI tools slash the cost of intellectual exploration—turning week-long research into hours—potentially flooding markets with nascent ideas and lowering development thresholds.

all.lede: Vestmark's TAMP platform hits $50B AUM milestone on retail UMA strength; Hayfin closes €15bn fund; Visa and Standard Chartered expand beyond core payments into fintech infrastructure.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP AUM surged 5x past $50B after launching retail UMA with tax management; major asset managers (T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, VanEck) now referring flow, signaling strong RIA tech demand.
Standard Chartered partnered with Circle to offer institutional clients direct USDC minting and redemption, expanding stablecoin infrastructure for digital asset trading.
Hayfin closed €15bn fifth direct lending fund with more conservative structuring after challenges with semi-liquid vehicles in US market.
Visa launched cyber threat intelligence platform for financial institutions and executed agentic commerce transactions in Europe, pushing deeper into software and services.
